central_question: If the police search your home illegally, can what they find still be used to convict you?
key_questions: What did Mapp v. Ohio actually hold, and on what constitutional basis (4th + 14th Amendment)?
What is the exclusionary rule and why did it not bind the states before 1961 (Wolf v. Colorado)?
What happened at Dollree Mapp's home in 1957 — kept strictly on the warrantless search, not the seized materials?
Why is the rule controversial (the 'constable has blundered' cost) and how was it later narrowed?
How does Mapp connect to Gideon and Miranda (incorporation / Warren Court)?
